sanskrit: सखेति मत्वा प्रसभं यदुक्तं हे कृष्ण हे यादव हे सखेति। अजानता महिमानं तवेदं मया प्रमादात्प्रणयेन वापि॥
transliteration: sakheti matvā prasabhaṃ yad uktaṃ he kṛṣṇa he yādava he sakheti | ajānatā mahimānaṃ tavedaṃ mayā pramādāt praṇayena vāpi ||
literal_meaning: Thinking (You) a friend, whatever I presumptuously said—‘O Kṛṣṇa, O Yādava, O friend’—not knowing this greatness of Yours, through carelessness or affection…
contextual_meaning: Arjuna experiences acute cognitive dissonance: the intimate friend he slapped on the back is suddenly the cosmic Absolute. He retroactively audits every casual word and gesture, now flooded with shame and corrective guilt.
psychological_state: Post-revelation shame-awe fusion; egoic self-image collapses as the former ‘equal’ realizes he was always dwarfed by the Other’s infinitude.
trigger_scenario: You discover your laid-back roommate is actually the CEO of the company you just mocked at happy hour; every joke you made now replays in cringe-inducing slow motion.
anchor_text: Speak to everyone as if the cosmos is listening.
